Thanks and just wanna send it out, but note that we will need to upload the meeting summary to GA.

The sentence is grammatically correct.
check grammar
I'm happy to check grammar! Please provide me with the text you would like me to review.

Thanks and just wanna send it out, but note that we will need to upload the meeting summary to GA.

Please let me know if we should upload the below (to be converted into PDF and upload to GA) after your review. Thanks.
The grammar in the provided text is correct. However, "GA" could be more clear - it might be helpful to specify what "GA" stands for.

Hi Team, 

Please note that the HK tax briefing is scheduled on June 26, 2023. We will update you one the briefing is conducted. 

check grammar

There is a small error in the grammar of this text. It should read:

"Please note that the HK tax briefing is scheduled for June 26, 2023. We will update you once the briefing has been conducted."

Please note that the HK tax briefing is scheduled to be conducted on June 26, 2023. We will update you one the briefing is conducted. 

check again

There is still a small error in the second sentence. It should read:

"We will update you once the briefing has been conducted."

Please note that the HK tax briefing is scheduled to be conducted on June 26, 2023. We will update you one the briefing has been conducted. 

check again

There is still a mistake in the second sentence. It should read:

"We will update you once the briefing has been conducted." 

Also, there is a minor issue in the first sentence. It would be more appropriate to use "scheduled for" instead of "scheduled to be conducted on". 

So the corrected sentence would be:

"Please note that the HK tax briefing is scheduled for June 26, 2023. We will update you once the briefing has been conducted."

Please note that the client's HK tax briefing is scheduled to be conducted on June 26, 2023. We will update you one the briefing has been conducted.

check again

There is still a mistake in the second sentence. It should be:

"We will update you once the briefing has been conducted." 

So the corrected text would be:

"Please note that the client's HK tax briefing is scheduled to be conducted on June 26, 2023. We will update you once the briefing has been conducted."

Please note that the client’s HK tax briefing is scheduled on June 26, 2023. We will update you once the briefing has been conducted.

check again
This sentence is grammatically correct.

Thank you for letting us know you have completed About Me. We will review the information provided and will let you know if we need further information from you. 

Please be advised that we are authorized by HSBC to prepare both of your 2022/23 Form IR56B and Individual Tax Return. We anticipate that the Form IR56B can be ready in July 2023. A copy of the Form IR56B will also be provided to you once it is ready. 


check grammar

The grammar in the provided text is correct. However, there are a few minor changes that might improve clarity:

"Thank you for letting us know you have completed your 'About Me.' We will review the information provided and let you know if we need any further information from you.

Please be advised that HSBC has authorized us to prepare both your 2022/23 Form IR56B and your Individual Tax Return. We anticipate that the Form IR56B will be ready in July 2023. Once it is ready, we will provide you with a copy of the Form IR56B."

Please be advised that HSBC authorized us to prepare both your 2022/23 Form IR56B and Individual Tax Return (“Tax Return”) and we will apply extension until August 3, 2023 to file your Tax Return, which means that you do not need to handle the Tax Return yourself and we will send the completed Tax Return for your review and signature once it is ready. Please note that further extension may be applied if necessary.
check grammar

The original text is generally grammatically correct. However, there are a few suggested changes for clarity:

"Please be advised that HSBC has authorized us to prepare both your 2022/23 Form IR56B and Individual Tax Return ("Tax Return"). We will also apply for an extension until August 3, 2023 to file your Tax Return, which means that you do not need to handle the Tax Return yourself. Once it is completed, we will send you the Tax Return for your review and signature. Please note that further extensions may be applied if necessary."

Please be advised that HSBC authorized us to prepare both your 2022/23 Form IR56B and Individual Tax Return (“Tax Return”) and we have applied extension until August 3, 2023 to file your Tax Return, which means that you do not need to handle the Tax Return yourself and we will send the completed Tax Return for your review and signature once it is ready. Please note that further extension may be applied if necessary.
check again
The revised text looks great and is grammatically correct. There are no further suggested changes.

For claiming the dependent allowances, please input the necessary information under “Dependents” section. For deductions to be claimed, please input the information under “Other expenses” section. Alternatively, you may also upload the receipts/statements in respect of the deductions for our handling. We will contact you separately if we need any further information from you.
please check

The grammar in the provided text is correct. However, there are a few minor changes that might improve clarity:

"To claim dependent allowances, please input the necessary information under the 'Dependents' section. For deductions to be claimed, please input the information under the 'Other Expenses' section. Alternatively, you may upload any receipts or statements related to the deductions for us to handle. We will contact you separately if we need any further information from you."

Thank you for your email and letting us know you are completing your GA About Me. 

Please find our comments in green embedded in your email below. Thanks.
